🛠️ Tools

AI Evaluation Tool — Complete Reference Guide

An interactive companion to the Lewis University Library AI Evaluation Tool — explore AI tools, teaching activities, and source materials

This tool was created using assistive AI for algorithmic construction, coding (e.g., building the HTML, UI, accessibility, etc.), and other elements outside the respective fields of the Research Team. Assistive AI usage conformed to an HCAI (Human-Centered AI) framework.

🎯 Open the AI Evaluation Tool →
🗺️
Welcome
Your Guide to the
AI Evaluation Tool Reference
This interactive reference is your companion to the AI Evaluation Tool. It houses all 33 AI tools, 500 activities, and 122 scholarly sources in one searchable, browseable interface. 💡 Swipe or use the arrows to walk through each feature.
🧭
Step 1 of 6
Navigate with the Command Hub
Below the header you'll find three large navigation buttons: 🛠️ Tools, 📋 Activities, and 📚 Sources. Click any button to switch between the AI tool catalog, the full activity library, and the scholarly bibliography. As you scroll, the sticky bar at the top displays which section you're currently viewing — it updates automatically when you switch sections. 💡 The indicator bubble in the sticky bar always shows your current section: Tools, Activities, or Sources.
🔍
Step 2 of 6
Search Everything
The large search bar in the hub lets you instantly filter across all tools, activities, and descriptions. As you scroll, a compact search field in the sticky bar stays available — both search inputs stay synced. Searching opens both the Tools and Activities sections simultaneously so you can see results from each. 💡 Try searching a keyword like "rubric," "ethics," or a tool name like "Claude."
🛠️
Step 3 of 6
Explore AI Tool Cards
The Tools tab displays all 33 AI tools organized into categories (General-Purpose, Research, Writing, Image Generation, etc.). Each card shows the tool's description, its pricing badge, and tags describing its best use-cases. Click a tool name to visit the tool's website. 💡 Tags like "discussion," "assessment," and "feedback" help you find tools by pedagogical function.
📋
Step 4 of 6
Browse 500 Activities
The Activities tab groups all 500 activities by category. Click a category header to expand it and reveal the activities inside. Each activity card shows its time estimate, Bloom's level, and associated AI tools. Click any activity card to reveal its full implementation instructions. 💡 Use search to narrow down activities across all categories at once — categories auto-expand to show matches.
📚
Step 5 of 6
Consult the Bibliography
The Sources tab contains all 122 scholarly references that informed the toolkit. Sources marked with a ✦ gold star are directly linked to individual activities through the toolkit's provenance system. 💡 Look for the 🔗 icon next to entries for direct links to the original publication.
🎯
Step 6 of 6
Return to the Evaluation Toolkit
This Reference Guide is a companion to the AI Evaluation Tool itself. Use the "← Back to Toolkit" button in the top navigation — or the link at the bottom of the page — to jump back to the main interactive tool with its carousel selection interface and full provenance system. 💡 The Toolkit and this Reference Guide share the same data — they're two views of the same resource.

This tool was created using assistive AI for algorithmic construction, coding (e.g., building the HTML, UI, accessibility, etc.), and other elements outside the respective fields of the Research Team. Assistive AI usage conformed to an HCAI (Human-Centered AI) framework.
